calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Nov-15-2007 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Judge Pro Tem David Commins, Clerk Carla Amador, Not Reported. (Department 525)

Appearances:
Appellant, Ronald Lew is present in propria persona.

Respondent, Nicole Kamatch is present in propria persona.
Respondents, Joyanne McClamroch and Matthew Lutz, are not present.

9:06 a.m.
This matter comes on calendar for Trial De Novo. The parties sign the stipulation to use Judge Pro Tem to hear the trial. Ronald Lew and Nicole Kamatch are sworn.

Respondent, Nicole Kamatch, presents her case in the narrative. Ms. Kamatch advises the Court that she was inform that Joyanne McClamroch and Matthre Lutz were not require to appear. Ms. Kamatch testifies as to each of the expenses contain in the summary of expenses. The respondent introduces the following documentary evidence:

Exhibit A - Summary of Expenses

Appellant, Ronald Lew, presents his case in the narrative. Judge Commins asks Mr. Lew questions as to the summary of expenses presented by Ms. Kamatch. Mr. lew makes his objections as to each of the items contain in the summary of expenses. The appellant introduces the following documentary evidence:

Exhibit 1 - Phone Call Logs.
Exhibit 2 - 4 (4 1/2 X 6) Colored Photographs.
Exhibit 3 - Notes of phone calls/conversations between Mr. Lew and Ms. Kamatch.
Exhibit 4 - Rental Agreement

The Court after hearing oral testimony from the parties, the Court takes the matter under submission. Appellant's and Respondent's exhibits from the Small Claims appeal returned to them.

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Feb-15-2007 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: 10:37am
Judge Pro Tem Paul Guiterrez, clerk-Beth Wilber, nothing on record:
Cause called for Small Claims appeal. Nicole J. Kamath appears as Pro Per respondent, there is no appearance by appellant, Ron M. Lew. Mr. Lew's previous motion to vacate judgment was denied. The appeal was for denial of motion to vacate and Judge Guiterrez denied the request. There being no appearance by Mr. Lew, or a timely and proper request for continuance filed, the matter is dismissed as abandoned by the appellant.
